what_it_does: Defines a React-style Stub component that renders a screen title and a centered placeholder panel for screens not implemented in the kit, then exposes the component on window.Stub.
observations: The file contains one component, Stub, with title and note props.
It renders an h1 using the cv-screen-title class.
The placeholder panel is styled with an inline style object using CSS custom properties for color, radius, and font.
A small uppercase label says Not implemented in this kit.
When note is absent, the component renders a default explanatory sentence using the title prop.
The component is assigned to window.Stub and is not exported as an ES module.
standouts: The component combines placeholder UI with implementation guidance in the default message.
Styling is local to the component except for the title class and design-token variables.
Global registration makes the component available as window.Stub.
conventions: Uses PascalCase naming for the component.
Uses JSX fragment wrapping for sibling elements.
Uses CSS variable tokens such as var(--bg-muted), var(--r-lg), var(--fg-secondary), var(--font-body), and var(--accent-bronze).
Uses inline styles for the placeholder panel and label.
Uses global window assignment rather than an import/export boundary.
concerns: If title is missing, the fallback note would include an undefined value.
Inline style objects make the visual treatment file-local and separate from stylesheet conventions.
The component has no prop validation or default title value.
notes: This file provides a reusable styled placeholder for unfinished screens and registers it globally.
questions: Is window.Stub the intended component registration mechanism for this kit?
Should title be required or given a fallback value?
Is the placeholder note intended to be visible to end users or only to kit implementers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a placeholder for screens that are not fully built out.
intention_signals: It appears intended to preserve a consistent screen shape for incomplete areas while giving a short cue about how those screens should be assembled later.
novelty: The fallback placeholder copy includes direct implementation guidance rather than only saying the screen is unavailable.
contribution: Contributes a neutral placeholder screen pattern with token-based styling, title display, optional note override, and a default message for unfinished kit surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: Stub.jsx defines a globally registered Stub component that renders a titled, styled placeholder panel for unimplemented screens.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for keeping unfinished screens presentable and directionally documented within the design system kit.

symbols.does: Renders a titled placeholder notice for an unfinished screen.
symbols.description: Stub accepts title and note props, displays the title as a screen heading, and shows a muted centered panel with a fixed label and either the provided note or a default explanatory message. It uses design-token CSS variables in inline styles and is exposed through window.Stub at the file level.
